region_of_origin: Japan / Australia (Wagyu cross-breed); dish prepared in Singapore
food_subgroup: Beef — grilled steak, high-marbling Wagyu striploin

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Wagyu striploin MBS 6-7 char-grilled. FOODON_03311434 (beef steak food product) is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Beef meat, loin) is the closest parent; no specific Wagyu MBS graded striploin code exists in FoodEx2 matrix.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226934003 (Beef steak) is the closest available parent; no Wagyu-specific or MBS-graded concept exists.

allergens: none identified (beef jus may contain gluten depending on preparation)

serving.description: 1 portion (250 g striploin steak with beef jus and roasted garlic)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is a 250-gram char-grilled Wagyu striploin steak served with beef jus and roasted garlic, as plated at the restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Beef, loin, top loin steak, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 1/8 inch fat, all grades, cooked, grilled — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #23383
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Wagyu MBS 6-7 striploin has significantly higher intramuscular fat than USDA standard striploin. USDA SR Legacy grilled top loin (lean and fat) values are used as the base, with fat and energy upward-adjusted to reflect Wagyu MBS 6-7 marbling characteristics documented in peer-reviewed literature (Gotoh et al., 2014, Meat Science; Smith et al., 2009, Meat Science). Protein is slightly lower in Wagyu due to fat displacement. Carbohydrate remains near zero. Values represent the striploin portion only; beef jus and roasted garlic contribute minor additional sodium and negligible macros at typical serving volumes and are noted separately.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database — pure beef / meat entries
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pure beef and grilled meat contain negligible digestible carbohydrate. The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed GI tables consistently assign GI values of 0 or effectively zero to pure meat products with no carbohydrate-containing accompaniments. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero, the lowest valid positive value) because carbohydrate_g per 100g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no glycemic carbohydrates present in plain grilled beef striploin). glycemic_load_per_serving = 0.0 because GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (1 × 0) / 100 = 0.0. gi_category is 'low' per convention for zero-carbohydrate meat foods.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from beef jus and natural meat sodium; high saturated fat may contribute to vascular stiffness
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that the beef jus accompaniment can add significant sodium beyond the 65 mg per 100 g in the meat itself; total dish sodium may reach 400–600 mg per serving, and portion control is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate content; high protein and fat with no glycemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Char-grilled Wagyu striploin contains zero digestible carbohydrate and will not raise postprandial blood glucose; however, the high saturated fat content (~22 g per 100 g) warrants moderation in individuals with type 2 diabetes who have concurrent dyslipidaemia.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (21.5 g/100 g) and saturated fat (8.8 g/100 g) from Wagyu MBS 6-7 marbling; elevated cholesterol (82 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high saturated fat and cholesterol content of MBS 6-7 Wagyu striploin is a significant concern for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease; consumption should be limited to occasional servings and balanced with vegetables and unsaturated fat sources.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Char-Grilled Wagyu Striploin MBS 6-7 (250 g) is a premium restaurant beef dish featuring a 250-gram Japanese Wagyu cross-breed striploin steak with a Marble Score of 6 to 7 on the Australian Meat Standards Australia (MSA) / AUS-MEAT scale, char-grilled over high heat and served with beef jus and roasted garlic. Wagyu cattle (and Wagyu cross-breeds) are selectively bred for exceptional intramuscular fat deposition, producing a steak with a rich, buttery flavour, tender texture, and visually distinctive white fat marbling throughout the muscle. MBS 6-7 represents a high marbling tier, significantly above commodity beef, resulting in a fat content of approximately 20–23 g per 100 g compared to 8–12 g in standard grilled striploin. The char-grilling method imparts a caramelised crust via Maillard reaction while preserving the juicy interior. Nutritionally, this dish is high in protein (~24.5 g/100 g), high in total fat (~21.5 g/100 g) including saturated fat (~8.8 g/100 g) and monounsaturated fat (~10.2 g/100 g), and contains essentially zero carbohydrate, making it glycemically inert. It is a rich source of zinc, vitamin B12, niacin, and selenium. The dish is clinically relevant for cardiovascular disease management due to its high saturated fat load, and should be consumed in moderation by individuals with dyslipidaemia. It is suitable for low-carbohydrate and ketogenic dietary patterns. At SGD 98 per serving, it is positioned as a premium fine-dining or casual-fine-dining item in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Wagyu striploin steak?
Is Wagyu steak good for diabetes?

confidence_reason: No approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central) contains a specific entry for Wagyu MBS 6-7 char-grilled striploin. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy grilled top loin (lean and fat) with fat and energy upward-adjusted based on peer-reviewed Wagyu marbling literature (Gotoh et al., 2014, Meat Science; Smith et al., 2009, Meat Science). Confidence is medium rather than low because the adaptation methodology is scientifically grounded and the base USDA data is from an approved primary source.
notes: 1. carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero — pure grilled beef striploin contains no digestible carbohydrate; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy beef loin entries and consistent with Wagyu beef composition literature. sugar_g is confirmed zero for the same reason. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as beef contains no dietary fibre. vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero as beef contains negligible vitamin C (below detection threshold in USDA data). 2. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this food contains zero glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category is 'low' per convention for zero-carbohydrate meat foods; glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 (GL = GI × carb_g_per_serving / 100 = 1 × 0 / 100 = 0.0). 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported for grilled beef striploin in the adapted USDA source entry used. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source. 4. Input cuisine tag 'Italian' and food_group 'Grains and staples' are incorrect for this dish; corrected to 'Western / Japanese Wagyu' and 'Meat and alternatives' respectively. 5. Beef jus and roasted garlic accompaniments contribute minor additional sodium (estimated 100–200 mg per serving from jus) and negligible macronutrients; per_100g values reflect the striploin steak only. 6. Halal status is not confirmed for this restaurant item; is_halal set to false pending verification. 7. trans_fat_g of 0.5 g/100 g reflects naturally occurring conjugated linoleic acid (CLA) and vaccenic acid trans fats present in ruminant beef fat, not industrial trans fats.

Added Malay, Tamil, and Hindi aliases to `aliases_by_language` and the flat `aliases` list to satisfy q8 alias coverage.
Changed `glycemic_index` from 0 to 1 as required by q4 glycemic rule for zero-carbohydrate foods, and updated `gi_source.source_match_notes` and `notes` accordingly.
Updated `veritas_qc` section to reflect current pass status and actions taken.
